root_cause.text: The maximum allowed value for `map_length` in the RLE image header was too large, allowing an attacker to cause an integer overflow when calculating the colormap size via a left shift operation (`1 << map_length`), leading to a memory allocation failure.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `coders/rle.c` lowers the maximum allowed `map_length` from 32 to 22 just before the shift operation (`map_length=one << map_length;`), and the commit message explicitly states "Lowered max map_length to prevent an overflow".

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The maximum allowed value for `map_length` in the RLE image header was too large, allowing an attacker to cause an integer overflow when calculating the colormap size via a left shift operation (`1 << map_length`), leading to a memory allocation failure.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `coders/rle.c` lowers the maximum allowed `map_length` from 32 to 22 just before the shift operation (`map_length=one << map_length;`), and the commit message explicitly states "Lowered max map_length to prevent an overflow".

intent_record.intent_summary: Tighten map_length validation to prevent integer overflow during bitwise shift.
intent_record.what: Changed the threshold for throwing an exception from map_length >= 32 to map_length >= 22.
intent_record.how: Restricts the maximum allowed value for map_length to prevent overflow or undefined behavior when performing a bitwise shift later in the function.

grouping_result.intent: Tighten map_length validation to prevent integer overflow during bitwise shift.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly tightens the validation of 'map_length' to prevent the integer overflow caused by the bitwise shift, which is the exact root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This validation allowed map_length to be up to 31, which is too large and led to an integer overflow during the bitwise shift.
This is the operation where the integer overflow occurs due to the improperly validated map_length.

vuln_statements.explanation: This check allowed map_length to be up to 31, which is too large and allows an integer overflow to occur later.
This is the actual bitwise shift operation where the integer overflow occurs due to the improperly validated map_length.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit aea6c6507f55632829e6432f8177a084a57c9fcc
Author: Cristy <urban-warrior@imagemagick.org>
Date:   Fri Oct 7 17:01:22 2016 -0400

    https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/issues/271

diff --git a/coders/rle.c b/coders/rle.c
index 8edd8133c..05a65ec22 100644
--- a/coders/rle.c
+++ b/coders/rle.c
@@ -224,7 +224,7 @@ static Image *ReadRLEImage(const ImageInfo *image_info,ExceptionInfo *exception)
     bits_per_pixel=(size_t) ReadBlobByte(image);
     number_colormaps=(size_t) ReadBlobByte
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit changes the validation check from 'map_length >= 64' to 'map_length >= 32', which properly prevents the integer overflow in the subsequent left shift operation 'map_length = one << map_length'.

--- a/coders/rle.c
+++ b/coders/rle.c
@@ -219,8 +219,11 @@ static Image *ReadRLEImage(const ImageInfo *image_info,ExceptionInfo *exception)
     number_planes=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
     bits_per_pixel=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
     number_colormaps=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
+    map_l
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces a bounds check `if (map_length >= 64)` before performing the left shift operation `one << map_length`, which prevents the integer overflow vulnerability.
